explanation: AddressSanitizer caught a read or write outside the bounds of a heap allocation — almost always an off-by-one in a loop, or an index computed wrong.
AddressSanitizer caught a read or write outside the bounds of a local (stack) array. Check your loop bounds and array sizes.
Your program touched memory after calling free() on it. The pointer is 'dangling' — it still holds an old address, but that memory is no longer yours.
UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer caught an array access outside the array's declared size — check the index expression and the array's bounds.
A signed integer calculation went past what an int can represent. In C this is undefined behavior, not a wraparound — the compiler is allowed to do anything.
Your program dereferenced a pointer that was NULL. Check that every pointer is actually pointing somewhere before you use *p or p->field.
